sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Right knee pain following a fall.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports right knee pain following an accidental fall while working on his property. He describes swelling and discomfort, with a pain score of 4/10. He has been taking ibuprofen for the swelling and pain, and reports a history of hypertension managed with lisinopril 20mg daily. He acknowledges a need to improve his diet and reduce salt intake to potentially reduce his medication dependence.
Findings
Physical examination reveals pain to palpation of the medial aspect of the right knee, limited range of motion with flexion and extension, and mild edema and effusion. The patient's blood pressure was 150/70 mmHg. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally, and the heart exam reveals a grade 2/6 systolic ejection murmur. An X-ray showed no fractures or bony abnormalities.
Assessment
The patient presents with a medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee. He also has hypertension.
Plan
medical_concepts: pain to palpation of the medial aspect of the right knee

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: Prescribe ibuprofen 800 mg twice daily for pain and swelling. Order a knee brace for support. Recommend ice therapy three to four times daily for 20 minutes. Refer the patient to a registered dietitian for dietary counseling. Continue lisinopril 20mg daily and schedule a follow-up appointment in four weeks to assess progress. Consider a referral to nutrition services to address dietary modifications and potential medication reduction.

medical_concepts: pain to palpation of the medial aspect of the right knee
